title: Understanding the Problem
text: The spider is hanging from a web that forms an angle of $$12$$ degrees below the horizontal on each side. We need to find the tension in the web.

title: Analyzing Forces
text: Consider the forces acting on the spider. The weight of the spider acts downward, and the tension in the web acts along the strands at an angle.

title: Components of Tension
text: The tension in the web has two components: a vertical component that balances the weight of the spider and a horizontal component that cancels out.

title: Calculating Vertical Component
text: The vertical component of the tension (T) can be expressed as T*sin(12 degrees). This component must equal the weight of the spider.

title: Solving for Tension
text: Rearrange the equation to solve for T.

title: Final Calculation
text: Calculate the value of T using the equation.
